provenance.author: Gemini Code Assist
provenance.context: A root-level semantic capsule describing the entire CobbleWright project structure and purpose.

intent.primary_goal: Provide a comprehensive, top-level overview of the CobbleWright project, its core components, architectural philosophy, and the roles of its main directories, so that AI agents and developers can understand the entire system at a glance.

identity.role: The master blueprint for the CobbleWright AI architect project.

priorities: The system is composed of a core runtime (`architect.js`), a modular `plugins` directory for features, a `data` directory for knowledge and state, and a `utils` directory for shared helpers.
Core orchestration logic resides in `architect.js`, which is kept intentionally thin. Its main job is to load packages.
Code is organized into `packages/`, with `packages/core` containing shared plugins/utils and `packages/agents/*` containing agent-specific logic.
All persistent knowledge and state is managed within the `data` directory, with a strict separation between static knowledge (`data/S.C`) and runtime artifacts (`data/chronoscribe`).
The project's vision, setup, and development guidelines are maintained in the `doc` directory.

never_do: Add feature logic directly to `architect.js` that belongs in a plugin.
Store runtime state files in the `data/S.C` knowledge directory.
Place one-off scripts or temporary files in the root directory.

folder_scope.purpose: The root directory for the CobbleWright project, an AI-powered companion bot for Minecraft that provides architectural advice and assistance.
document_types: Subdirectories for plugins, data, utils, and documentation.

architecture.entry_point: `architect.js` is the main application entry point. It initializes the bot, loads configuration, loads all semantic capsules from `data/S.C`, loads all plugins from `plugins`, and connects to the Minecraft server.
architecture.packages_directory: The `packages/` folder contains all source code, organized into a `core` package for shared logic and an `agents` package for specialized agent behaviors (e.g., `agents/prime`).
architecture.data_directory: The `data` folder is for all persistent data. It contains the `S.C` subdirectory for the AI's core knowledge base (semantic capsules) and the `chronoscribe` subdirectory for the immutable audit log. See `data-overview.sc.json` for details.
architecture.utils_directory: Shared, reusable helper functions are located in `packages/core/utils/`. See `utils-overview.sc.json` for details.
architecture.sc_overview_directory: The `sc-overview` folder contains human-readable, agent-generated semantic capsules that describe the structure and purpose of other project directories. These are for documentation and developer reference, and are not loaded by the runtime.
architecture.doc_directory: The `doc` folder contains all project documentation, including setup guides (`SETUP.md`), the project's philosophical intent (`INTENT.md`), and the strategic playbook (`PLAYBOOK.md`).
architecture.MinecraftServer_directory: A self-contained, pre-configured Minecraft server for local development and testing. It is configured for `online-mode=false` to allow the bot to connect.
architecture.tests_directory: Contains the Jest test suite for the project, with tests organized to mirror the `plugins` and `utils` directories.
architecture.dashboard_directory: A placeholder for a potential future web-based dashboard for monitoring the bot's status and performance.

runtime_guidance.for_prompts: Start with this capsule to get a high-level understanding of the project. From here, navigate to the more specific overview capsules for the relevant subdirectories (`plugins`, `data`, etc.).
runtime_guidance.for_drafting: When adding a new file, use this capsule to decide which directory it belongs in based on its function (e.g., a new command goes in `plugins`, a new helper function goes in `utils`).
runtime_guidance.for_maintenance: Keep the `architecture` section of this capsule updated if major new directories are added or the core structure of the project changes.

trust.note: This is the root capsule for the entire project, synthesized from all other sub-capsules and documentation. It should be considered the most authoritative source for the overall project structure.
